sprint: Creator Signature Sky Challenge — Measured Fit, Not clamp()-Only Sizing
task: Root-caused a fresh 'widget still has scroll' report: the sky-recognition challenge relied entirely on CSS clamp() sizing, unlike the sibling star-tap-grid screen which has always used a real measure-and-shrink fit function. Added _fitSkyChallengeToAvailableSpace, mirroring _fitBoardToAvailableSpace's own discipline: shrinks Lumo's portrait, then the sky-card grid, then (via a new --sky-density custom property) every remaining fixed margin/padding on the screen, solved analytically rather than guessed. Verified via Playwright across 10 realistic viewport sizes (420x500 to 1920x1080): zero overflow everywhere, versus real measured overflow at several before the fix; extreme sizes correctly degrade to the existing overflow-y:auto safety net. Full regression across both existing sky-challenge suites passes unchanged.
